comment:
## Deliverables Complete

All three deliverables for [POL-30](/POL/issues/POL-30) are finished. Parent: [POL-26](/POL/issues/POL-26).

---

### 1. Code Review & Familiarization

Read through the full codebase — all runtime modules (DialogueEngine, DialogueRunner, DialogueUI, DialogueParser, Theme, Types), the Designer plugin (Plugin, Canvas, NodeWidget, ConnectionRenderer, AppState, PropertyPanel, Serializer, Toolbar, DesignerTheme), examples, templates, build scripts, and all documentation.

**Architecture summary:**
- **Runtime:** DialogueEngine is the main facade (singleton). DialogueRunner handles tree traversal, conditions, visited state. DialogueUI handles ScreenGui, typewriter, input, portraits, animations. DialogueParser converts text format to tree tables. Theme provides 5 presets + merge/extend utilities.
- **Designer:** Signal-driven MVC pattern. AppState is single source of truth with undo/redo via deep-copy snapshots. Canvas manages the scrollable node graph. NodeWidget renders individual cards. ConnectionRenderer draws lines. PropertyPanel edits the selected node. Serializer handles bidirectional text-format conversion. Toolbar provides import/export/save.
- **Data model:** Types.lua defines clean types for trees, nodes, choices, conditions, actions, themes, callbacks, saved state.

**Existing CODE_REVIEW.md is accurate.** The critical connection leak (issue #1) should be fixed before launch — it causes orphaned input listeners that can close new dialogues. The fix (`_disconnectNodeConnections`) has already been implemented in the current DialogueUI.lua. Medium issues (RegisterTree visited reset, voice sound Once, dead imports, LoadState docs, singleton docs) are good polish candidates.

---

### 2. Icon & Feature Images

All assets generated and saved to `memories/DialogueEngine/assets/`:

**Icon:** `icon-dialogue-pro.jpg`
- Gold/amber speech bubble with white branching node-graph connectors on deep navy background
- Clean, professional, immediately reads as 'dialogue + branching flow'
- Suitable for 512x512 Creator Store icon

**Feature Images (5):**
1. `feature1-node-editor.jpg` — Visual node editor canvas with connected dialogue nodes (green start, gray normal, red end)
2. `feature2-runtime-ui.jpg` — Runtime dialogue UI in a fantasy RPG village with NPC portrait, typewriter text, numbered choice buttons
3. `feature3-themes.jpg` — All 5 built-in themes side by side (Dark, Fantasy, Sci-Fi, Bubblegum, Horror)
4. `feature4-text-visual.jpg` — 'Two Ways to Write Dialogue' — text format on left, visual node graph on right with bidirectional arrows
5. `feature5-features-overview.jpg` — 4-quadrant feature showcase: branching conditions, quest flags/actions, multi-input support, save/load progress

---

### 3. Pricing Analysis — $10 USD Evaluation

**Competitive landscape research:**

| Product | Price | Features |
|---|---|---|
| Advanced NPC Dialogue System (Payhip) | $20 USD | Branching trees, custom UI, animations, facial expressions |
| NPC Quest + Combat V2 (BuiltByBit) | $26 USD | NPC + quest + combat bundle |
| Advanced Quest System (BuiltByBit) | $1.49 USD | Basic quest system only |
| Roblox Dialogue Editor (Plugin) | Free | Basic dialogue creation, dated (2017) |
| Dialogue Maker (GitHub) | Free | Open source, basic |
| Built-in Roblox Dialog | Free | Legacy, very limited |
| Various free models | Free | Low quality, no theming, no visual editor |

**Key differentiators we have that no competitor offers:**
- Free visual Designer plugin companion (no other system has this)
- 5 built-in theme presets with Theme.Extend() API
- Simple text format + Lua table format (dual authoring)
- Full actions/flags system built into dialogue (no external code needed)
- Variable interpolation and inline text control ([pause], [speed])
- Comprehensive demo (7-NPC Thornwood Village)
- Auto-viewport NPC portraits
- Full keyboard, gamepad, touch support

**$10 USD verdict: Well-positioned and recommended.**

- **Below the only comparable paid system** ($20 Advanced NPC Dialogue) — immediately competitive
- **Above commodity pricing** ($1.49 quest systems) — signals quality without intimidating
- **The free Designer plugin adds enormous perceived value** — buyers feel they're getting two products for the price of one
- **$10 is the sweet spot** for Roblox developer tools: accessible for hobbyists, credible for studios
- **Previous recommendation was 1,400 Robux (~$17.50 USD)** — the board's $10 decision is more aggressive but should drive higher adoption volume

**Optional strategies:**
- Launch at **$7.99** for 30 days to build reviews and DevForum buzz, then raise to $10-$12
- Or hold at **$10 permanent** as the 'accessible premium' — volume over margin
- Consider a **$14.99 'Pro' tier** later if you add features like multi-tree management or analytics

---

**Note:** Checkout returned 500 Internal Server Error on all attempts (not 409). This appears to be a server-side issue. Proceeding with work despite checkout failure.
